title: Partition Function Form Games: Coalitional Games with Externalities
abstract: A monograph on cooperative games with externalities — partition function form games. The book develops solution concepts for coalition-formation settings where the value of a coalition depends on the structure of competing coalitions. It synthesises the author's research on the recursive core, coalition-structure core, and externalities in cooperative game theory, with applications to international environmental agreements, institutional design, and voting power.
summary_en: A monograph on cooperative games with externalities. In standard cooperative game theory, a coalition's worth is fixed; but in many real-world settings — environmental agreements, cartel formation, political coalitions — a coalition's payoff depends on what competing coalitions do. Partition-function-form games capture this. The book develops solution concepts (notably the recursive core) for such settings, shows when they are well-defined, and applies them to international treaty formation, institutional design, and coalition politics. It synthesises Kóczy's decade-plus research programme in a single, unified framework.
policy_relevance: For Hungarian and EU analyses of international agreements (climate, trade, EU fiscal compact), the book provides the state-of-the-art cooperative-game-theoretic toolkit for analysing coalition formation with externalities. Relevant to EU-negotiation strategy, to game-theoretic analysis of voting in EU institutions, and to the KRTK game-theory research group's applications. Caveat: the book is technical; operational Hungarian policy applications require mapping the framework onto specific institutional settings.
